summary: Sign-out navigation bug is NOT fixed. Contrary to the review request's claim that 'signOut now calls router.replace("/(auth)/login") after supabase.auth.signOut', the actual signOut in /app/frontend/src/lib/auth-context.tsx (lines 62-65) still only calls supabase.auth.signOut() and setProfile(null) — no navigation is triggered. Additionally, the file was CORRUPTED with a garbage duplicate suffix at lines 71-74 that produced a Babel SyntaxError and caused the Metro bundle to return 500 (entire app rendered a blank white screen). Testing agent trimmed the file back to 70 valid lines so the app could load, then reproduced the reported user bug end-to-end: signed up a fresh Supabase user (testsignout_1783230265@example.com / TestPass123!), reached /(customer)/account, tapped [data-testid='signout-button'], and observed the app stayed on /account with the bottom tab bar still visible and the login screen never appeared (login-email-input.is_visible() = False; URL remained /account). The Supabase signup trigger is now working — a user_profiles row with role='customer' was auto-created, so the signup SQL fix mentioned in the review appears already applied.

ui_bugs.issue: signOut does not call router.replace('/(auth)/login'). After tapping Sign out on any tab (customer/seller/admin), app stays inside the authenticated tab group (URL /account, /overview, etc.) and the login screen is never shown. Repro shows 'Guest' label appears but tab bar remains. Reference: /app/frontend/src/lib/auth-context.tsx:62-65

File contained a corrupted trailing suffix (lines 71-74: 'ding, refreshProfile, signOut }}>...' + duplicate export) that produced a Babel SyntaxError at 71:30 and caused entry.bundle to return HTTP 500 → blank white screen for entire app. Testing agent trimmed the file to 70 lines so bundling succeeded; main agent should verify no other files were corrupted by the same tool that produced this artifact.

integration_issues.issue: supabase.auth.signOut() succeeds and onAuthStateChange fires with session=null, but the (customer)/(seller)/(admin) route groups have no session-guard that redirects to /(auth)/login when session becomes null. Only app/index.tsx redirects based on session, and it is not re-mounted when a user is already deep in a tab route. Fix options: (a) call router.replace('/(auth)/login') at the end of signOut, or (b) add a guard useEffect in each of (customer)/_layout.tsx, (seller)/_layout.tsx, (admin)/_layout.tsx that watches session and redirects to /(auth)/login when null.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' during login submission — coming from RN internals / expo-router. Non-blocking but should be cleaned up.

action_items: MUST-FIX: Make signOut actually navigate to /(auth)/login. Either add `router.replace('/(auth)/login')` at the end of signOut() in /app/frontend/src/lib/auth-context.tsx (after `setProfile(null)`), or add a session-guard in each authenticated layout ((customer)/_layout.tsx, (seller)/_layout.tsx, (admin)/_layout.tsx) that redirects when session===null. Recommended: add it in signOut for the direct fix AND also add a layout guard for defense-in-depth (so an expired session on refresh also routes to login).

Verify no other files in /app/frontend/src/ contain the same trailing-garbage corruption artifact (grep for stray '}};' or duplicate exports). auth-context.tsx had the pattern which shows a prior search_replace/write operation was truncated. Testing agent fixed only this file.

Only customer sign-out was reproduced end-to-end. Seller and Admin sign-out could not be tested without creating users of those roles, but they all call the same `signOut` from useAuth and are broken by the same root cause. Retest all three roles once the fix is applied.

Consider re-enabling email confirmation in Supabase Auth settings — signup currently returns an access_token immediately with email_verified=true, which suggests confirmation is disabled. Not blocking, but production-security concern.
critical_code_review_comments: auth-context.tsx signOut is a no-op for navigation — the review request explicitly says the fix was applied, but grep shows no router.replace call anywhere in that function. Main agent should re-verify their diff was actually saved.

index.tsx redirect is the only session guard in the app. Any user already inside (customer)/(seller)/(admin) after sign-out will remain there because Expo Router does not re-mount index.tsx on state change. This is a common Expo Router pitfall.

auth-context.tsx contained a syntactically broken duplicate suffix that broke the ENTIRE app bundle (500 on entry.bundle → blank screen). This should have been caught by main agent before handing off — running expo/metro locally or a simple `tsc --noEmit` would have flagged it.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/lib/auth-context.tsx (trimmed corrupted lines 71-74; NO business-logic change — did NOT add the missing router.replace call because that is the main agent's fix to make)

success_rate.frontend: 0% (0/3 sign-out flows work — customer verified broken; seller/admin blocked by same root cause)

seed_data_creation: Created Supabase auth user testsignout_1783230265@example.com / TestPass123! (role=customer, auto-created user_profiles row via signup trigger). Safe to reuse for regression.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test credentials: testsignout_1783230265@example.com / TestPass123! (customer). To test seller/admin sign-out, create Supabase users and update user_profiles.role via Supabase SQL editor. Main flow: login → /(customer)/home → tap 'Account' tab → tap [data-testid=signout-button] → EXPECT to land on /(auth)/login with [data-testid=login-email-input] visible. Currently FAILS: URL stays at /account and tab bar remains visible with 'Guest' label.
rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE: /app/frontend/src/lib/auth-context.tsx signOut() function only clears local auth state; it never navigates. Expo Router does not automatically re-evaluate route guards on session change, so users deep inside (customer)/(seller)/(admin) route groups remain on those screens after supabase.auth.signOut() succeeds. REPRO: (1) sign in as any user, (2) navigate to Account tab, (3) tap Sign out, (4) observe URL unchanged and login screen NOT shown. MITIGATION: Add `router.replace('/(auth)/login')` inside signOut() OR add a session-guard useEffect in each authenticated tab _layout.tsx that redirects when session is null. Additionally, file was corrupted with a duplicated JSX/export suffix causing Metro bundle SyntaxError — fixed by testing agent by truncating to line 70.
